narrative:
The regime sits firmly in the business as usual quadrant, with a stress score of 14 out of 100 signalling a calm and well-functioning market environment. Broad conditions are supportive: equity returns are positive, credit markets are not flashing warnings, and there is no meaningful sign of systemic pressure building beneath the surface.

The VIX at 17.0 sits at the 38th percentile of its ten-year range, which is neither elevated nor unusually suppressed, and the contango term structure confirms that options markets are not pricing near-term tail risk. High yield spreads at 272 basis points are at just the 8th percentile of their five-year history, with a z-score of negative 1.11, while investment grade sits at 74 basis points on a similarly tight reading; credit is unambiguously confirming the calm equity picture rather than contradicting it.

XLE has shed 4.5% over the past five days and XLP is down 2.6%, suggesting some rotation away from energy and defensives, while technology has led with XLK up 6.2% and EEM gaining 5.3% alongside a 3.3% rise in EWG. The divergence between growth-oriented and defensive sectors points to a risk-on tilt in positioning, with investors appearing to reduce hedges and increase exposure to cyclical and technology names.
